paper_title: The influences of oral communication strategies on students' English speaking proficiency

entities.description: The main field of research focusing on teaching English to non-native speakers.
The ability of students to communicate effectively in spoken English.
Intentional techniques used by learners to overcome communication difficulties.
Researcher affiliated with the Faculty of Foreign Languages, UNETI.

entities.id: University of Economics – Technology for Industries (UNETI)
entities.description: The university where the authors are affiliated and where the study was conducted.
A survey instrument adapted from Nakatani (2005) to measure communication strategies.
A methodology combining quantitative (questionnaire) and qualitative (interviews) data.
Statistical software used to analyze the quantitative data collected.
Reliability coefficient determining the internal consistency of the questionnaire.
The highest-ranked communication strategy used by students.

entities.description: The third most preferred approach used by the students.
